ddls:
CREATE TABLE "respondents" (
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each respondent */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the respondent */,
  "class_of_worker" INTEGER /* Class of worker (e.g., private, government, self-employed) */,
  "industry_code" INTEGER /* Industry code (e.g., agriculture, construction, manufacturing) */,
  "occupation_code" INTEGER /* Occupation code (e.g., management, sales, service) */,
  "education" INTEGER /* Education level (e.g., high school, bachelor's degree, master's degree) */,
  "wage_per_hour" REAL /* Wage or salary per hour */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the respondent */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth for more accurate age calculation */,
  "ethnicity" TEXT /* Detailed ethnicity information */,
  "disability_status" INTEGER /* Indicates if the respondent has a disability */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Record creation timestamp */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Record last update timestamp */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "demographics" (
  "demographic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each demographic record */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "marital_status" INTEGER /* Marital status (e.g., married, single, divorced) */,
  "major_industry_code" INTEGER /* Major industry code (e.g., agriculture, construction, manufacturing) */,
  "major_occupation_code" INTEGER /* Major occupation code (e.g., management, sales, service) */,
  "race" INTEGER /* Race or ethnicity */,
  "hispanic_origin" INTEGER /* Hispanic origin (yes/no) */,
  "household_size" INTEGER /* Number of people in the household */,
  "home_ownership" TEXT /* Ownership status of the home (e.g., owned, rented) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geographic region (e.g., Northeast, Midwest) */,
  "urban_rural_status" TEXT /* Indicates if the respondent lives in an urban or rural area */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("demographic_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_demographics_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employment" (
  "employment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employment record */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "member_of_labor_union" INTEGER /* Member of a labor union (yes/no) */,
  "reason_for_unemployment" INTEGER /* Reason for unemployment (e.g., laid off, quit, new job) */,
  "full_or_part_time_employment_stat" INTEGER /* Full-time or part-time employment status */,
  "capital_gains" REAL /* Capital gains */,
  "capital_losses" REAL /* Capital losses */,
  "dividends_from_stocks" REAL /* Dividends from stocks */,
  "employment_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of current employment in months */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Specific job title of the respondent */,
  "work_hours_per_week" INTEGER /* Average hours worked per week */,
  "employment_sector" TEXT /* Sector of employment (e.g., public, private, non-profit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employment_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "migration" (
  "migration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each migration record */,
  "respondent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the respondent */,
  "live_in_this_house_1_year_ago" INTEGER /* Lived in this house 1 year ago (yes/no) */,
  "migration_prev_res_in_sunbelt" INTEGER /* Previous residence in sunbelt region (yes/no) */,
  "num_persons_worked_for_employer" INTEGER /* Number of persons worked for employer */,
  "family_members_under_18" INTEGER /* Number of family members under 18 */,
  "country_of_birth_father" INTEGER /* Country of birth of father */,
  "country_of_birth_mother" INTEGER /* Country of birth of mother */,
  "country_of_birth_self" INTEGER /* Country of birth of self */,
  "citizenship" INTEGER /* Citizenship status */,
  "migration_year" INTEGER /* Year of migration */,
  "reason_for_migration" TEXT /* Reason for migrating (e.g., job, family, education) */,
  "previous_residence_state" TEXT /* State of previous residence */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("migration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_migration_respondent_id FOREIGN KEY ("respondent_id") REFERENCES respondents ("respondent_id")
)
column_names: Class of worker (e.g., private, government, self-employed)
